author: codex
rationale: The repo already separates canonical checked-in knowledge from generated machine state. Codex memory should follow the same pattern so it stays durable, reviewable, and rebuildable.
statement: Store canonical Codex memory in docs/codex_memory and derived retrieval state in work/codex_memory.

author: codex
rationale: The repo needed one explicit implementation direction that matches the current planning baseline and removes ambiguity left by older C++ or CMake-oriented guidance. Anchoring the first package on foundation tooling preserves the evidence-first workflow and keeps later runtime slices fed by reusable parsers, fixtures, validators, and diagnostics.
statement: Use Zig 0.15.2 with SDL2 as the canonical LBA2 port runtime target and stage implementation through a Foundation + asset CLI first package before viewer and gameplay slices.

author: codex
rationale: Phase 1 planning needed one frozen evidence baseline so future implementation work would not re-decide canonical roots, source owners, target fixtures, or where generated state belongs. Keeping the baseline split between checked-in docs and rebuildable work output matches the repo's current-state policy and the existing mbn_workbench pattern.
statement: Use docs/phase0 as the canonical checked-in phase 0 baseline and work/phase0 as the only supported generated surface for asset inventory, source ownership, and golden-target evidence regeneration.

author: codex
rationale: Phase 0 already proves the player slot and loader position but not a direct scene-level body or animation mapping. Human sign-off would not add evidence, while a guessed mapping would violate the repo's current-state and fail-fast policy. The correct next step is deeper evidence gathering only if the implementation boundary needs that exact linkage.
statement: Treat the SCENE.HQR entry 2 player actor body/animation linkage as an explicit evidence gap that does not currently require human verification; resolve it only with stronger tool-backed source or asset evidence if later implementation work depends on it.

author: codex
rationale: Phase 1 needed the planned workspace to exist as real code instead of remaining only a placeholder and prose boundary. Rooting the workspace directly at port/ keeps one canonical implementation path, lets the asset CLI own deterministic generated outputs under work/port/phase1, and gives later decoding slices stable parsers, fixtures, and validation hooks without adding compatibility shims.
statement: Implement the canonical Phase 1 LBA2 port workspace directly under port/ with two Zig executables, lba2 and lba2-tool, and treat work/port/phase1 as the only supported generated surface for Phase 1 catalog and fixture outputs.

author: codex
rationale: Real-asset validation exposed container behavior that the initial simplified assumptions did not cover. Recording those surprises in one checked-in memo reduces the chance of future sessions reintroducing monotonic-offset assumptions, misreading the semantic RESS.HQR[49] target, or treating the SDL2 linker issue as an application-code defect.
statement: Treat the Phase 1 implementation memo as the checked-in summary of the current workspace state, including that SDL2 linking is an environment dependency on this machine and that HQR parsing must support repeated offsets and the semantic-to-physical RESS.HQR movie-index mapping.

author: codex
rationale: The workspace already carries a repo-local SDL2 install under vcpkg_installed/x64-windows, and relying on ambient system PATH state was the only reason the smoke app could not be verified earlier. Wiring the build directly to that checked-in location preserves one canonical dependency path, avoids fallback discovery logic, and makes zig build run deterministic on this machine.
statement: Resolve the Phase 1 SDL2 dependency from the repo-local vcpkg_installed/x64-windows layout and install SDL2.dll beside the emitted app so zig build run does not depend on ambient system library discovery.
